title: Hardcoded Credentials
description:
An admin endpoint compares the supplied API key against a secret hardcoded directly in the source file. Anyone who reads the source code (e.g. via a public repo or a leak) immediately has the secret.

Fix `check_api_key()` to read the secret from an environment variable instead.
hint: Use `os.environ.get('ADMIN_API_KEY')` and ensure the comparison uses a constant-time function like `hmac.compare_digest()` to prevent timing attacks.
explanation: Hardcoded secrets are frequently exposed through version control history, logs, or code leaks. Always store secrets in environment variables or a secrets manager. Use `hmac.compare_digest()` for constant-time comparison to prevent timing oracle attacks.

attack_flow.label: Attacker reads
attack_flow.value: Key extracted from Git history, leaked file, or public repo

attack_flow.label: Result
attack_flow.value: Admin endpoint unlocked — sensitive data returned

why_blocked: Reading the key from an environment variable (`os.environ.get('ADMIN_API_KEY')`) means the secret is never in source code. Even if the codebase is fully public, an attacker cannot know the key without access to the deployment environment.
